title: SprintService.loadSprintIssues — API error from board discovery not propagated to test coverage
description: SprintService.discoverBoard (lines 171-209) has a specific branch at line 204 where `getBoards()` returns `ApiResult.Error` — it logs and returns null, which causes `loadSprintIssues` to return `NOT_FOUND`. The SprintServiceTest only covers the 'no boards in success response' case (line 63: `ApiResult.Success(emptyList())`), not the 'API itself failed' case. Similarly, the fallback path `loadScrumBoardIssues` → `loadKanbanBoardIssues` when `getActiveSprints` returns an `ApiResult.Error` (lines 68-73) is exercised by zero tests — the existing fallback test (line 71) only covers the `ApiResult.Success(emptyList())` case.
suggestedFix: Add to SprintServiceTest: (1) `loadSprintIssues returns error when getBoards itself fails` — stub `getBoards` to return `ApiResult.Error(ErrorType.NETWORK_ERROR, "Cannot reach Jira")`, assert result is `isError`. (2) `loadSprintIssues falls back to board issues when getActiveSprints returns API error` — stub `getActiveSprints(1)` to return `ApiResult.Error(ErrorType.SERVER_ERROR, "500")`, stub `getBoardIssues(1)` to return issues, assert success and `activeSprint == null`. Both use `runTest` + MockK, no platform required.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed by reading SprintServiceTest.kt and SprintService.kt. The SprintServiceTest has 6 tests, none of which cover: (1) getBoards() returning ApiResult.Error — SprintService.kt lines 204-207 log the error and return null, causing loadSprintIssues to return NOT_FOUND; the test at line 62 only tests Success(emptyList()). (2) getActiveSprints(boardId) returning ApiResult.Error in loadScrumBoardIssues — SprintService.kt lines 68-73 fall back to loadKanbanBoardIssues; the test at line 71 uses ApiResult.Success(emptyList()) not ApiResult.Error. Both are real untested branches. Severity is Medium not High: the code paths are simple delegations with correct fallback logic already written; the risk is regression if someone modifies the fallback logic without noticing the gap, but there is no current observable bug.
recommendedAction: Add two SprintServiceTest cases: (1) stub getBoards() to return ApiResult.Error and assert loadSprintIssues returns isError; (2) stub getActiveSprints(1) to return ApiResult.Error, stub getBoardIssues(1) to return issues, and assert success with activeSprint == null.

title: SprintService.loadAvailableSprints and loadIssuesForSprint — completely untested
description: SprintService exposes two public methods with no test at all: `loadAvailableSprints(boardId)` (lines 106-115) calls `getActiveSprints` and `fetchRecentClosedSprints`, merges results, and sorts by endDate. `loadIssuesForSprint(sprintId, allUsers)` (lines 160-167) updates `cachedIssues` and returns the sprint issues. Both are called from UI panels and are state-mutating. `fetchRecentClosedSprints` itself runs a pagination loop against `getClosedSprints` and reads/writes `SprintPaginationCache` — that loop has no test coverage whatsoever.
evidence:
suspend fun loadAvailableSprints(boardId: Int): List<JiraSprint> {
    val activeResult = apiClient.getActiveSprints(boardId)
    val active = (activeResult as? ApiResult.Success)?.data ?: emptyList()
    val recentClosed = fetchRecentClosedSprints(boardId, count = 3)
suggestedFix: Add SprintServiceTest cases: (1) `loadAvailableSprints returns active sprints followed by sorted recent closed` — stub `getActiveSprints` returning one sprint, stub `getClosedSprints` returning a single `isLast=true` page with two closed sprints. Assert the returned list is active-first then sorted by `endDate` descending. (2) `loadIssuesForSprint returns issues and updates cachedIssues` — stub `getSprintIssues(99, false)`, assert result and `getCachedIssues()`. (3) `loadAvailableSprints handles getClosedSprints API error gracefully` — stub `getClosedSprints` returning an error, assert `loadAvailableSprints` returns just the active sprint without throwing.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed by reading SprintServiceTest.kt. No test references loadAvailableSprints, loadIssuesForSprint, or fetchRecentClosedSprints. SprintService.kt lines 106-115 (loadAvailableSprints), 129-154 (fetchRecentClosedSprints with its pagination loop), and 160-167 (loadIssuesForSprint) are entirely uncovered. fetchRecentClosedSprints is a non-trivial pagination loop that reads/writes SprintPaginationCache and has a while(true) break condition — it is a meaningful coverage gap. These are called from UI panels and are state-mutating (cachedIssues). Severity is Medium: the logic works based on code inspection, but the gap leaves pagination regressions silent.
recommendedAction: Add SprintServiceTest cases for loadAvailableSprints (active + sorted closed), loadIssuesForSprint (stub getSprintIssues, assert cachedIssues updated), and fetchRecentClosedSprints error handling (getClosedSprints returning error should return empty list without throwing).

title: AttachmentDownloadService — entirely untested, including error responses, path-traversal guard, and parallel concurrency limit
description: AttachmentDownloadService has no tests. Key untested paths: (1) `downloadAttachment` with blank content URL returns null (line 51). (2) Non-2xx HTTP response deletes temp dir and returns null (lines 67-70). (3) Path-traversal guard: a filename like `../../etc/passwd` is blocked at line 82-85 (canonicalPath check). (4) Empty response body returns null (lines 73-76). (5) `downloadAll` concurrency limit (Semaphore(3)) with 5+ attachments. (6) `downloadThumbnail` cache hit returns same object without making a network call.
suggestedFix: Add `AttachmentDownloadServiceTest` using `MockWebServer` + `@TempDir`: (1) `downloadAttachment returns null for blank URL`. (2) `downloadAttachment returns null on 4xx response`. (3) `downloadAttachment path-traversal filename returns null` — use filename `../../secret`. (4) `downloadAttachment happy path writes bytes to target dir`. (5) `downloadAll with 5 attachments makes at most 3 concurrent requests` — inject a latched mock to observe concurrency. (6) `downloadThumbnail caches result and skips second HTTP request`. Use `runTest` + `Dispatchers.IO` override via `TestDispatcher`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed: no test file references AttachmentDownloadService anywhere. AttachmentDownloadService.kt covers: blank URL guard (line 50-52), non-2xx response handling (lines 67-70), empty body guard (lines 73-76), path-traversal guard (lines 82-85), concurrency limit via Semaphore(3) (line 119), and thumbnail LRU cache (line 138). All are untested. Severity is Medium (not Low) because of the security-relevant path-traversal guard at lines 82-85: a regression there (e.g., someone simplifying the filename sanitization without running tests) would allow a malicious Jira attachment to write files outside the target directory. The path-traversal check uses canonicalPath comparison after replacing ".." with "_" — this deserves a pinning test. The concurrency limit and cache are correctness concerns but not security-impacting.
recommendedAction: Add AttachmentDownloadServiceTest using MockWebServer + @TempDir. Priority: (1) path-traversal filename returns null — this has security implications and must be pinned; (2) blank URL returns null; (3) non-2xx returns null; (4) happy path writes bytes; (5) downloadThumbnail caches result and skips second HTTP call. The concurrency limit test is desirable but lower priority.

title: JiraApiClient.getWorklogs — mid-pagination API error path not tested
description: JiraApiClient.getWorklogs (lines 567-588) uses a `while` loop and short-circuits with `return r` on the first `ApiResult.Error` from any page (line 578). This path — where page 1 succeeds but page 2 returns a 4xx/5xx — is not tested. The existing JiraServiceImplTest tests only cover the 2-page success case and a single-page parse. A mid-pagination auth failure would silently return an error discarding all already-collected entries, which callers may not handle gracefully.
evidence:
is ApiResult.Error -> return r
is ApiResult.Success -> r.data
suggestedFix: Add to JiraServiceImplTest (using MockWebServer): `getWorklogs returns error immediately when second page fails` — enqueue a valid first page (total=200, worklogs=[100 items]), then enqueue a 401 response. Assert result is `isError` with `ErrorType.AUTH_FAILED`. Also add: `getWorklogs returns empty list when server returns total=0` — enqueue `{"total":0, "worklogs":[]}`, assert empty `isSuccess`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real coverage gap. JiraApiClient.getWorklogs (lines 567-588) paginates via a while loop and at line 578 does 'is ApiResult.Error -> return r', discarding any already-collected entries and returning the error immediately. The existing JiraServiceImplTest covers the 2-page success case (page 1: 100 items, page 2: 2 items) and the single-page author-username case, but no test enqueues a successful first page followed by a 4xx/5xx second page. The mid-pagination error path is real and observable: a 401 on page 2 silently discards the collected page-1 entries and returns an error, which callers may not handle gracefully (e.g., showing an error to the user when partial data was available). Severity is correctly Medium.
recommendedAction: Add to JiraServiceImplTest (or a new JiraApiClientWorklogPaginationTest): enqueue a valid first page (total=200, 100 worklogs) then a 401 response; assert result.isError with ErrorType.AUTH_FAILED. Optionally also test total=0 returns an empty success list.

title: JiraTransitionResponseParser.parse — malformed/missing 'to' field and empty transitions body not tested
description: JiraTransitionResponseParser.parse uses `!!` dereferences on `n["to"]!!`, `to["id"]!!`, and `to["name"]!!` (lines 28-30) and on `n["id"]!!` and `n["name"]!!` (lines 38-39). If the Jira server returns a transition without a `to` field (which can happen for internal admin transitions), the parser will throw a `NullPointerException` inside `getTransitions`, crashing the caller. The test suite only covers well-formed transitions; no test verifies behavior when a required field is absent.
evidence:
private fun parseTransition(n: JsonObject): TransitionMeta {
    val to = n["to"]!!.jsonObject
    val toStatus = StatusRef(
        id = to["id"]!!.jsonPrimitive.content,
        name = to["name"]!!.jsonPrimitive.content,
suggestedFix: Add to JiraTransitionResponseParserTest: (1) `parse skips or throws on transition missing to field` — body with a transition where `to` is absent; assert either an exception is caught (and document the decision) or that the entry is skipped. If the policy is to throw, wrap the call site in a try/catch and convert to `ApiResult.Error`. (2) `parse returns empty list for empty transitions array` — body `{"transitions":[]}`. (3) `parse returns empty list when transitions key is missing entirely` — body `{}`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real crash risk. JiraTransitionResponseParser.parseTransition (lines 27-43) uses n["to"]!!.jsonObject (line 28), to["id"]!! (line 30), to["name"]!! (line 31), n["id"]!! (line 37), and n["name"]!! (line 38). Kotlin's !! throws NullPointerException when the value is null or absent. JiraTransitionResponseParserTest only tests well-formed transitions with all fields present. A Jira server returning a transition without a 'to' field (internal admin transitions are documented to sometimes lack this) would NPE inside the arr.map{} call at line 24, propagating up to getTransitions and crashing the caller. No try/catch wraps the parse call site. The suggested fix (decide throw-and-catch vs skip policy) is straightforward and non-breaking.
recommendedAction: Either add a try/catch around parseTransition and skip malformed entries (logging a warning), or wrap the parse call site in getTransitions with try/catch converting to ApiResult.Error. Then add JiraTransitionResponseParserTest cases: (1) transition missing 'to' field — assert either exception caught and empty list returned or ApiResult.Error at call site; (2) empty transitions array returns empty list; (3) missing 'transitions' key returns empty list (already handled by the ?: return emptyList() at line 23).

title: SprintPaginationCache — entirely untested, including corruption recovery and atomic-move path
description: SprintPaginationCache is a singleton object with file-based persistence, an in-memory lock, LRU-style pagination hints, corrupt-file recovery, and atomic-move writes. None of these paths have a single test. Key missing scenarios: (1) first call returns 0 when no file exists; (2) `saveCachedStartAt` then `getCachedStartAt` round-trips correctly with `pageSize` subtraction; (3) corrupt JSON on disk triggers delete-and-reset instead of crashing; (4) `getCachedStartAt` on a board with `lastStartAt < pageSize` returns 0 (coerceAtLeast guard).
suggestedFix: Add a `SprintPaginationCacheTest` using a `@TempDir` to redirect `cacheDir`. Tests: (1) `getCachedStartAt returns 0 on first call` (no file). (2) `save then getCachedStartAt returns lastStartAt minus pageSize, floored at 0`. (3) `getCachedStartAt with lastStartAt < pageSize returns 0`. (4) `corrupt file is deleted and cache returns 0` — write invalid JSON to the file, then call `getCachedStartAt`, assert 0 and file is gone. All are pure Kotlin/JUnit5 with temp dirs — no IntelliJ platform needed.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed: no test file references SprintPaginationCache anywhere. The class (SprintPaginationCache.kt lines 1-129) is a singleton object with file-based persistence, synchronized cache, corrupt-file recovery, and atomic-move writes. All paths described in the finding are real and untested. However, severity should be Low rather than Medium: SprintPaginationCache is a pure performance hint cache — a wrong value produces at most an extra API round-trip on the first call after a reset. The data it caches is non-critical pagination offsets; the fallback (startAt=0) is handled correctly at line 53 via the null coalescing return 0. Corrupt file is caught by a try/catch at line 102 that deletes and returns fresh CacheData. No data loss or incorrect behavior results from cache misses.
recommendedAction: Add a SprintPaginationCacheTest with a @TempDir. Cover: getCachedStartAt returns 0 on first call (no file); save/load round-trip with pageSize subtraction; lastStartAt < pageSize returns 0; corrupt JSON file is deleted and 0 returned. Pure Kotlin/JUnit5, no IntelliJ platform needed.

title: TicketKeyCache — entirely untested including LRU eviction, regex fallback, and API error path
description: TicketKeyCache has zero tests. Critical uncovered paths: (1) `extractKeys` with the default regex against multi-key text, with the MAX_REGEX_MATCHES=200 cap, and with a syntactically invalid custom regex (PatternSyntaxException fallback). (2) `validateAndCache` when `validateTicketKeys` returns `ApiResult.Error` — invalid keys must still not appear in cache, and the method must not throw. (3) `evictIfNeeded` at exactly MAX_SIZE+1 entries — eviction uses `cache.keys().toList()` ordering which is non-deterministic in ConcurrentHashMap, so the eviction count must match regardless of which key is dropped.
suggestedFix: Add `TicketKeyCacheTest` (pure Kotlin, no platform): (1) `extractKeys finds all Jira-style keys in text` — text with 3 keys + noise. (2) `extractKeys falls back to default regex on invalid pattern` — inject invalid pattern via ConnectionSettings mock or test constructor. (3) `extractKeys caps at MAX_REGEX_MATCHES` — pass text with 201 keys, assert `size == 200`. (4) `validateAndCache caches valid keys and nulls for invalid ones` — stub `validateTicketKeys` returning only subset, assert missing keys map to `null`. (5) `validateAndCache API error leaves cache empty for that batch`. (6) `evictIfNeeded keeps cache at MAX_SIZE after overfill` — add 501 entries, assert `cache.size == 500`.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed: no test file references TicketKeyCache. TicketKeyCache.kt lines 35-53 (extractKeys with regex fallback and MAX_REGEX_MATCHES cap), lines 70-90 (validateAndCache including ApiResult.Error path), and lines 97-103 (evictIfNeeded) are all untested. The finding is accurate. Severity is Low not Medium: TicketKeyCache is used for hyperlinking ticket keys in descriptions — a UI enhancement. On extraction failure or API error, the service degrades gracefully: extractKeys falls back to DEFAULT_REGEX (line 50), validateAndCache on error logs and returns without caching (lines 86-88), and the cache serves as an optimization only. The eviction logic is simple (take(toRemove) from a ConcurrentHashMap snapshot) and already functions correctly without a test. No user-impacting bug exists.
recommendedAction: Add a TicketKeyCacheTest (pure Kotlin, no platform): cover extractKeys with valid keys, invalid custom regex fallback, MAX_REGEX_MATCHES cap, validateAndCache on ApiResult.Success and ApiResult.Error, and evictIfNeeded at MAX_SIZE+1 entries.

title: JiraApiClient.searchIssues — validation branches (too-long text, control chars, key-pattern JQL switch) have no tests
description: JiraApiClient.searchIssues (lines 104-127) has three defensive branches with zero test coverage: (1) `text.length > 500` returns `VALIDATION_ERROR` immediately without an HTTP call. (2) `text.any { it.code < 32 }` returns `VALIDATION_ERROR` for control characters. (3) `looksLikeKey` regex (line 115) switches the JQL to `text ~ "…" OR key = "…"` form — only the non-key free-text form is implicitly exercised via other tests. The internal `escapeJql` function (lines 993-1001) is also completely untested for its reserved-character escaping logic.
suggestedFix: Add to JiraApiClientTest (using MockWebServer): (1) `searchIssues returns VALIDATION_ERROR and no HTTP call for text over 500 chars`. (2) `searchIssues returns VALIDATION_ERROR and no HTTP call for text with control character`. (3) `searchIssues uses key OR text JQL form when text matches Jira key pattern` — assert the request path contains `key+%3D`. (4) Add a standalone `EscapeJqlTest` (pure Kotlin): verify `+`, `-`, `"`, `(` each escape to `\+`, `\-`, `\"`, `\(`, and that plain alphanumeric text is unchanged.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real coverage gap. JiraApiClient.searchIssues (lines 104-127) has three defensive branches with no tests: (1) text.length > MAX_SEARCH_TEXT_LENGTH (line 108) returns VALIDATION_ERROR with no HTTP call; (2) text.any { it.code < 32 } (line 111) returns VALIDATION_ERROR; (3) looksLikeKey regex (line 115) switches JQL to key= OR text~ form. A search across all test files found zero references to searchIssues, escapeJql, or MAX_SEARCH_TEXT_LENGTH. The JiraServiceImplSearchTicketsValidationTest tests searchTickets (a different JiraServiceImpl method) not the JiraApiClient.searchIssues method directly. The escapeJql function at lines 993-1001 is also entirely untested. Severity is Medium per the finding but is better assessed as Low: the validation logic itself is straightforward defensive guards that are very unlikely to regress silently, and callers pass user-typed text so the validation being untested doesn't constitute an observable correctness defect in production. Adding tests is pure upside with no risk.
recommendedAction: Add MockWebServer tests to a new JiraApiClientSearchIssuesTest: (1) text over 500 chars returns VALIDATION_ERROR with no HTTP request; (2) text with a control character (code < 32) returns VALIDATION_ERROR; (3) text matching [A-Z][A-Z0-9]+-\d+ produces JQL containing 'key =' in the request URL. Add a standalone pure-Kotlin EscapeJqlTest verifying that +, -, ", ( escape to \+, \-, \", \( and plain alphanumeric is unchanged.

title: JiraApiClient.getRawString — 429 RATE_LIMITED and non-JSON content-type (PARSE_ERROR) not tested in any helper path
description: The `checkJsonContentType` guard on `getRawString` (lines 337-362) maps a 200 + non-JSON Content-Type (e.g. `application/xml` or `text/plain`) to `PARSE_ERROR`. This path is tested only via the `text/html` → `AUTH_FAILED` path in JiraApiClientTest (for the typed `get` helper). No test covers `getRawString` receiving a `text/xml` or `application/xml` body, nor does any test exercise the `429 RATE_LIMITED` response code for any method despite it being a distinct branch in all five HTTP helpers.
suggestedFix: Add to JiraApiClientTest: (1) `getRawString 200 with application/xml content-type maps to PARSE_ERROR` — enqueue a 200 + `Content-Type: application/xml` + XML body; assert `ErrorType.PARSE_ERROR`. (2) `getBoards 429 maps to RATE_LIMITED` — enqueue `MockResponse().setResponseCode(429)`; assert `ErrorType.RATE_LIMITED`. The 429 test can use any method since all map via the shared `get` helper.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real coverage gap, but severity should be Low rather than Medium. The checkJsonContentType function (lines 855-873) maps a 200 + non-JSON Content-Type to PARSE_ERROR for all HTTP helpers including getRawString. JiraApiClientTest tests the text/html -> AUTH_FAILED path only for getCurrentUser (which uses the typed get helper). JiraApiClientSameOriginTest tests getRawString only for same-origin guarding. No test exercises: (a) getRawString returning 200 with application/xml Content-Type -> PARSE_ERROR; (b) 429 -> RATE_LIMITED for any method. Both branches exist and work correctly in code (getRawString line 351; checkJsonContentType line 865); tests would merely provide regression coverage. 429 is rare in practice for a self-hosted Jira DC instance, and the non-JSON Content-Type path is well-covered by the shared checkJsonContentType function tested via other helpers. The risk of silent regression is real but low.
recommendedAction: Add to JiraApiClientTest or JiraApiClientSameOriginTest: (1) getRawString with 200 + Content-Type: application/xml maps to PARSE_ERROR; (2) getBoards (or any method using the typed get helper) with 429 maps to ErrorType.RATE_LIMITED.

title: BranchingService.fetchLinkedBranches — dev-status fallback to Bitbucket search has no test
description: BranchingService.fetchLinkedBranches (lines 59-80) has two branches: primary dev-status API (returns non-empty) and fallback Bitbucket branch-name search when dev-status returns empty or an error. Neither branch has a test. The fallback is important for repositories not linked to Jira's dev-status system, and any regression would silently return an empty list to the Start Work dialog's branch picker.
suggestedFix: Add to BranchingServiceTest: (1) `fetchLinkedBranches returns dev-status branches when available` — stub `getDevStatusBranches(issue.id)` to return two branches, stub `allBranches` as empty; assert the returned list matches the dev-status data. (2) `fetchLinkedBranches falls back to Bitbucket name filter when dev-status returns empty` — stub `getDevStatusBranches` to return empty list, provide `allBranches` with one matching and one non-matching entry; assert only the matching branch is returned. Both are pure MockK + `runTest`, no platform needed.
verdictReasoning: Confirmed real coverage gap. BranchingServiceTest only tests the 'no silent fallback' contract for useExistingBranch and startWork (the localVcsRootPath mismatch -> NOT_FOUND path). The fetchLinkedBranches method (lines 59-80) has two branches — primary dev-status API (non-empty result) and Bitbucket name-search fallback (empty/error dev-status) — neither has any test. A regression in the fallback filter logic (e.g., case sensitivity change in the contains() call at line 74) would silently return an empty list to the Start Work dialog's branch picker. Severity is correctly Low since the logic is simple (a filter and a map), the fallback is informational rather than destructive, and the impact is UX degradation (empty picker) not data loss.
recommendedAction: Add to BranchingServiceTest using MockK: (1) fetchLinkedBranches returns dev-status branches when getDevStatusBranches returns non-empty; (2) fetchLinkedBranches falls back to Bitbucket name filter when dev-status returns empty, returning only branches whose displayId contains issue.key. Both are pure runTest+MockK, no platform needed.
